title: Cyanotis kewensis
taxonomy: Cyanotis kewensis is classified within the family Commelinaceae, which comprises a group of flowering plants commonly known as spiderworts. It belongs to the genus Cyanotis, a group of tropical plants native to Africa and Asia. Cyanotis kewensis is further classified as a species within the genus, Cyanotis. The species is native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent. It is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and delicate flowers.

intro: Cyanotis kewensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae, commonly known as the blue star grass. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyanotis kewensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and as a natural remedy for respiratory issues.
